contract.title: Freelance Service Agreement (California) — Brand Refresh 2025
contract.preamble: This Freelance Service Agreement (the "Agreement") is entered into as of 2025-01-15 (the "Effective Date") by and between TechStartup Inc., with Sarah Johnson, Marketing Director, as authorized representative ("Client"), and Chen Creative Studio, with Alex Chen, Senior Designer, as authorized representative ("Freelancer"). Client and Freelancer may be referred to individually as a "Party" and collectively as the "Parties."
contract.recitals:
WHEREAS, Client desires to engage Freelancer to provide professional design services for a complete visual identity redesign for Client’s "Brand Refresh 2025" project, including logo, color palette, typography, and a brand guidelines document; and
WHEREAS, Freelancer represents that Freelancer has the skill, experience, and ability to perform the services described herein; and
WHEREAS, the Parties desire to set forth the terms and conditions governing the services, deliverables, payment, and ownership of work product.
NOW, THEREFORE, in consideration of the mutual covenants and promises herein, the Parties agree as follows:

clauses.title: Definitions
clauses.content:
1.1 "Services" means the professional design and related services to be performed by Freelancer as described in this Agreement and any incorporated exhibit(s).
1.2 "Deliverables" means the tangible and intangible work product to be delivered to Client as listed in Section 2.
1.3 "Final Deliverables" means Deliverables for which Client has accepted the applicable milestone and made the corresponding payment in full.
1.4 "Client Materials" means all materials, information, content, trademarks, logos, brand assets, and other items provided by Client to Freelancer.
1.5 "Freelancer Tools" means pre-existing materials, templates, know-how, techniques, skills, software, and workflows owned or licensed by Freelancer and not uniquely created for Client.
1.6 "Work Product" includes Deliverables and other work created by Freelancer in performing Services.

clauses.title: Scope of Services; Deliverables; Milestones
clauses.content:
2.1 Project. Freelancer will perform a complete visual identity redesign for "Brand Refresh 2025," including logo, color palette, typography selections, and a brand guidelines document.

2.2 Deliverables and Milestone Schedule. Freelancer will provide the following Deliverables (each a "Milestone"), subject to Client’s timely feedback and approvals:
(a) Logo concepts (3 options) — due Jan 30, 2025 — $2,000.
(b) Selected logo refinement — due Feb 15, 2025 — $1,500.
(c) Brand guidelines document — due Mar 1, 2025 — $2,500.
(d) Asset package delivery — due Mar 15, 2025 — $2,500.

2.3 Deadline. The Parties intend to complete the project by 2025-03-15 (the "Deadline"). The Deadline is dependent on Client’s timely provision of Client Materials and timely review/approval. If Client delays approvals, feedback, or provision of materials, the schedule will be extended by a period reasonably necessary for Freelancer to perform.

2.4 Exclusions. Unless expressly included in Section 2.2 or a written change order, Services do not include printing, web/app development, motion design, photography/videography, copywriting, trademark registration/legal clearance, paid stock licensing fees, or ongoing brand management.

2.5 Acceptance. Each Milestone Deliverable will be deemed accepted upon the earliest of: (i) Client’s written approval; (ii) Client’s use in commerce or publication; or (iii) five (5) business days after delivery if Client does not provide written notice of material nonconformity and reasonable detail within that period. If Client timely rejects for material nonconformity, Freelancer will correct and re-submit within a reasonable time, and acceptance testing will repeat.

clauses.title: Client Responsibilities
clauses.content:
3.1 Client will provide timely access to decision-makers, brand strategy inputs, existing assets, and feedback needed to perform Services.
3.2 Client will ensure that Client Materials are accurate and that Client has the right to provide and authorize Freelancer’s use of Client Materials.
3.3 Client will provide consolidated feedback and approvals (preferably in writing) and will designate a single point of contact authorized to approve Deliverables on Client’s behalf.
3.4 Client will review Deliverables promptly and, in any event, within the timelines in Section 2.5, to avoid schedule impacts.

clauses.title: Fees; Deposit; Payment Schedule; Invoicing
clauses.content:
4.1 Total Amount. The total fee for the Services is $8,500.00 (the "Total Amount"), exclusive of approved reimbursable expenses.

4.2 Deposit. Client will pay a deposit of $2,550.00 upon execution of this Agreement (the "Deposit"). The Deposit is applied toward the Total Amount.

4.3 Milestone Payments. The Parties agree to a milestone payment schedule tied to the Deliverables in Section 2.2. Client will pay the applicable milestone amount upon delivery of the milestone Deliverable and Freelancer’s invoice, unless otherwise stated below.

4.4 Allocation and Credit of Deposit. The Deposit will be credited against the last milestone(s) due, unless otherwise agreed in writing.

4.5 Invoicing and Payment Due Date. Freelancer will invoice for the Deposit upon signing and for each Milestone upon delivery. Client will pay each undisputed invoice within ten (10) calendar days of the invoice date.

4.6 Late Payments. Overdue amounts accrue interest at the lesser of (i) 1.5% per month, or (ii) the maximum rate permitted by California law, from the due date until paid. Client will reimburse reasonable costs of collection, including reasonable attorneys’ fees and costs, to the extent permitted by law.

4.7 Suspension for Nonpayment. If any undisputed invoice remains unpaid ten (10) days after written notice of delinquency, Freelancer may suspend work until payment is received, and the schedule will be extended accordingly.

clauses.title: Revisions; Change Orders
clauses.content:
5.1 Included Revisions. The Total Amount includes up to three (3) rounds of revisions ("Revision Rounds") for the project. A "round" means a single consolidated set of requested changes to a Deliverable based on Client’s review.

5.2 Out-of-Scope Changes. Requests that materially change direction, require additional concepts beyond those listed, introduce new deliverables, or exceed Revision Rounds are out of scope and will require a written change order.

5.3 Change Orders and Additional Fees. Out-of-scope work will be billed at Freelancer’s then-current hourly rate or a fixed fee as agreed in a written change order. Freelancer is not obligated to begin out-of-scope work until the Parties sign the change order (email confirmation is acceptable) and Client pays any required advance.

5.4 Client Delay Impacts. If Client requests changes after approving a Milestone, such changes constitute out-of-scope work unless the Parties agree otherwise in writing.

clauses.title: Expenses and Third-Party Costs
clauses.content:
6.1 Reimbursable Expenses. Client will reimburse pre-approved, reasonable out-of-pocket expenses incurred for the project (e.g., stock imagery/fonts/licenses), provided Freelancer obtains Client’s written approval before incurring any single expense over $100.

6.2 Third-Party Terms. Any third-party licenses are subject to the third party’s terms. Freelancer will transfer to Client any license documentation in Freelancer’s possession for approved third-party assets. Unless expressly agreed, Freelancer does not provide legal advice regarding license scope or compliance.

clauses.title: Intellectual Property; Ownership; License
clauses.content:
7.1 Ownership of Client Materials. Client retains all right, title, and interest in Client Materials. Client grants Freelancer a limited, nonexclusive, royalty-free license to use Client Materials solely to perform the Services.

7.2 Ownership of Work Product (Upon Full Payment). Upon Client’s full payment of all amounts due under this Agreement, including milestone fees and approved expenses, Freelancer hereby assigns to Client all right, title, and interest in and to the Final Deliverables created specifically for Client under this Agreement, excluding Freelancer Tools and excluding any third-party materials.

7.3 Freelancer Tools. Freelancer retains all right, title, and interest in Freelancer Tools. To the extent any Freelancer Tools are incorporated into the Deliverables, Freelancer grants Client a perpetual, nonexclusive, worldwide, royalty-free license to use such incorporated Freelancer Tools solely as part of the Deliverables for Client’s internal and external business purposes.

7.4 Preliminary Works. Drafts, concepts not selected, and exploratory work (including unused logo concepts) remain Freelancer’s property; however, Client may internally evaluate such materials during the project. Client may not use or publish unpurchased or unapproved concepts without a separate written license from Freelancer.

7.5 Moral Rights. To the maximum extent permitted by applicable law, Freelancer waives and agrees not to assert any "moral rights" (including rights of attribution and integrity) in the Final Deliverables assigned to Client.

7.6 Portfolio Use (Credit). Unless Client provides written objection for confidential reasons, Freelancer may display the Deliverables and project name in Freelancer’s portfolio, website, and marketing materials after public launch by Client, provided Freelancer does not disclose Client confidential information. Client may request reasonable credit placement where customary.

7.7 No Trademark Clearance. Client acknowledges that Freelancer is not providing trademark search/clearance or legal advice. Client is responsible for confirming that any final branding is available and non-infringing.

clauses.title: Confidentiality
clauses.content:
8.1 Confidential Information. "Confidential Information" means non-public information disclosed by either Party that is designated as confidential or that reasonably should be understood to be confidential given the nature of the information and circumstances of disclosure.

8.2 Obligations. The receiving Party will: (i) use Confidential Information only to perform or receive benefits under this Agreement; (ii) not disclose Confidential Information to any third party except to its employees/contractors who have a need to know and are bound by confidentiality obligations at least as protective; and (iii) protect Confidential Information using reasonable care.

8.3 Exclusions. Confidential Information does not include information that: (i) is or becomes publicly available through no breach; (ii) was known by the receiving Party prior to disclosure; (iii) is received from a third party without breach; or (iv) is independently developed without use of the disclosing Party’s Confidential Information.

8.4 Required Disclosure. A Party may disclose Confidential Information to the extent required by law, subpoena, or court order, provided it gives prompt notice (to the extent legally permitted) and cooperates reasonably in seeking protective treatment.

8.5 Duration. Confidentiality obligations continue during the term and for three (3) years after termination, except for trade secrets, which will be protected for as long as they remain trade secrets under applicable law.

clauses.title: Representations and Warranties
clauses.content:
9.1 Mutual. Each Party represents that it has the full right, power, and authority to enter into and perform this Agreement.

9.2 Freelancer Warranties. Freelancer warrants that: (i) the Deliverables will be original to Freelancer except for Client Materials and properly licensed third-party materials; and (ii) Freelancer will perform Services in a professional and workmanlike manner consistent with generally accepted industry standards.

9.3 Disclaimer. EXCEPT AS EXPRESSLY STATED IN THIS AGREEMENT, FREELANCER DISCLAIMS ALL OTHER WARRANTIES, EXPRESS OR IMPLIED, INCLUDING IMPLIED WARRANTIES OF MERCHANTABILITY, FITNESS FOR A PARTICULAR PURPOSE, AND NON-INFRINGEMENT.

9.4 Client Warranties. Client warrants that Client has the rights necessary to provide Client Materials and to authorize their use as contemplated herein.

clauses.title: Indemnification
clauses.content:
10.1 Client Indemnity. Client will defend, indemnify, and hold harmless Freelancer and its officers, employees, and contractors from and against any third-party claims, damages, liabilities, costs, and expenses (including reasonable attorneys’ fees) arising out of or related to: (i) Client Materials; (ii) Client’s use of the Deliverables in violation of this Agreement; or (iii) Client’s breach of this Agreement.

10.2 Freelancer Indemnity. Freelancer will defend, indemnify, and hold harmless Client and its officers, employees, and contractors from and against any third-party claims that the Final Deliverables (excluding Client Materials and third-party materials) infringe a U.S. copyright or U.S. trademark, provided that Client: (i) promptly notifies Freelancer in writing of the claim; (ii) allows Freelancer sole control of the defense and settlement (provided no settlement admits fault by Client or imposes obligations on Client without Client’s consent, not to be unreasonably withheld); and (iii) cooperates reasonably at Freelancer’s expense.

10.3 Remedies. If a Deliverable becomes, or in Freelancer’s reasonable opinion is likely to become, the subject of an infringement claim, Freelancer may, at its option: (i) procure for Client the right to continue using it; (ii) replace or modify it to be non-infringing while maintaining substantially similar function and design intent; or (iii) if (i) and (ii) are not commercially reasonable, terminate the affected portion of the Services and refund fees paid for the infringing portion of the Final Deliverables.

10.4 Exclusions. Freelancer has no indemnity obligation to the extent a claim arises from: (i) Client Materials; (ii) modifications by anyone other than Freelancer; (iii) use outside the scope of this Agreement; (iv) combination with materials not provided by Freelancer; or (v) Client’s failure to use updated or corrected versions provided by Freelancer.

10.5 Sole Remedy. This Section states each Party’s sole and exclusive remedies for third-party intellectual property infringement claims.

clauses.title: Limitation of Liability
clauses.content:
11.1 Limitation. TO THE MAXIMUM EXTENT PERMITTED BY LAW, IN NO EVENT WILL EITHER PARTY BE LIABLE FOR ANY INDIRECT, INCIDENTAL, SPECIAL, CONSEQUENTIAL, OR PUNITIVE DAMAGES, OR FOR LOST PROFITS, LOST REVENUE, LOSS OF DATA, OR BUSINESS INTERRUPTION, ARISING OUT OF OR RELATED TO THIS AGREEMENT, EVEN IF ADVISED OF THE POSSIBILITY OF SUCH DAMAGES.

11.2 Cap. TO THE MAXIMUM EXTENT PERMITTED BY LAW, EACH PARTY’S TOTAL LIABILITY ARISING OUT OF OR RELATED TO THIS AGREEMENT WILL NOT EXCEED THE TOTAL AMOUNT PAID OR PAYABLE BY CLIENT TO FREELANCER UNDER THIS AGREEMENT ($8,500), EXCEPT THAT THIS CAP WILL NOT APPLY TO: (i) CLIENT’S PAYMENT OBLIGATIONS; (ii) BREACH OF CONFIDENTIALITY; OR (iii) INDEMNIFICATION OBLIGATIONS TO THE EXTENT PROHIBITED FROM LIMITATION UNDER APPLICABLE LAW.

11.3 Allocation. The Parties acknowledge that the fees reflect the allocation of risk and the limitations set forth herein.

clauses.title: Term; Termination; Kill Fee
clauses.content:
12.1 Term. This Agreement begins on the Effective Date and continues until completion of the Services, unless earlier terminated in accordance with this Section.

12.2 Termination for Convenience. Either Party may terminate this Agreement for convenience upon seven (7) days’ written notice.

12.3 Termination for Cause. Either Party may terminate immediately upon written notice if the other Party materially breaches this Agreement and fails to cure within five (5) business days after written notice specifying the breach.

12.4 Effect of Termination; Payment. Upon termination, Client will pay Freelancer for: (i) all Services performed through the termination effective date; (ii) any Milestones delivered but unpaid; (iii) approved expenses incurred; and (iv) if termination is for convenience by Client after work has begun, a reasonable wind-down/administrative fee equal to 10% of the unpaid portion of the Total Amount (the "Kill Fee"), not to exceed $850.

12.5 Deposit; No Refund. The Deposit is non-refundable once work has commenced, except to the extent required by law.

12.6 Delivery of Work in Progress. Upon Client’s request and payment of all amounts then due, Freelancer will provide Client with copies of work-in-progress files in a reasonably accessible format; however, Client acknowledges that editable source files may include Freelancer Tools and may not be released unless expressly agreed in writing.

12.7 Survival. Sections concerning payment obligations, confidentiality, intellectual property/ownership, indemnification, limitation of liability, dispute resolution, and other provisions that by their nature should survive will survive termination.

clauses.title: Independent Contractor; No Employment Relationship
clauses.content:
13.1 Independent Contractor. Freelancer is an independent contractor and not an employee, agent, partner, or joint venturer of Client. Freelancer has no authority to bind Client.

13.2 Taxes and Benefits. Freelancer is solely responsible for all taxes, withholdings, insurance, and benefits applicable to Freelancer and its personnel.

13.3 Control of Work. Freelancer retains control over the manner and means of performing the Services, subject to the requirements of this Agreement and Client’s acceptance of Deliverables.

clauses.title: Compliance with Law
clauses.content: Each Party will comply with applicable federal, state, and local laws, rules, and regulations in connection with its performance under this Agreement.

clauses.title: Dispute Resolution; Attorneys’ Fees
clauses.content:
15.1 Good-Faith Negotiation. The Parties will first attempt in good faith to resolve any dispute arising out of or relating to this Agreement through informal discussions between authorized representatives.

15.2 Mediation. If the dispute is not resolved within fifteen (15) calendar days, either Party may request non-binding mediation in California with a mutually agreed mediator. The Parties will share mediator fees equally.

15.3 Litigation; Venue. If mediation does not resolve the dispute, either Party may bring an action in the state or federal courts located in California, and each Party consents to such courts’ personal jurisdiction.

15.4 Attorneys’ Fees. The prevailing Party in any action or proceeding arising out of or relating to this Agreement will be entitled to recover its reasonable attorneys’ fees and costs, to the extent permitted by law.

clauses.title: Notices
clauses.content: All notices must be in writing and will be deemed given when delivered personally, sent by confirmed email, or sent by nationally recognized overnight courier to the addresses or emails the Parties designate in writing. Either Party may update its notice information by written notice to the other Party.

clauses.title: Force Majeure
clauses.content: Neither Party will be liable for any delay or failure to perform (excluding payment obligations) due to events beyond its reasonable control, including acts of God, natural disasters, war, terrorism, labor disputes, utility or internet failures, governmental orders, or pandemics, provided the affected Party gives prompt notice and uses reasonable efforts to resume performance.

clauses.title: Assignment; Subcontracting
clauses.content:
18.1 Assignment. Neither Party may assign this Agreement without the other Party’s prior written consent, except that either Party may assign this Agreement in connection with a merger, acquisition, or sale of substantially all assets, provided the assignee agrees in writing to be bound.

18.2 Subcontracting. Freelancer may use qualified subcontractors to perform portions of the Services, provided Freelancer remains responsible for performance and for ensuring subcontractors comply with confidentiality obligations.

clauses.title: Publicity
clauses.content: Except for Freelancer’s portfolio rights in Section 7.6, neither Party will issue press releases or public announcements referencing the other Party or the project without the other Party’s prior written consent, not to be unreasonably withheld.

clauses.title: Miscellaneous (Entire Agreement; Amendments; Severability; Counterparts; Electronic Signatures)
clauses.content:
20.1 Entire Agreement. This Agreement, including any exhibits or change orders, constitutes the entire agreement between the Parties regarding the subject matter and supersedes all prior and contemporaneous understandings.

20.2 Amendments. Any amendment or modification must be in writing and signed by both Parties. Email or e-signature confirmations are acceptable if they evidence mutual assent.

20.3 Severability. If any provision is held invalid or unenforceable, the remaining provisions will remain in full force, and the invalid provision will be reformed to the minimum extent necessary to make it enforceable.

20.4 Waiver. No waiver is effective unless in writing, and a waiver of one breach is not a waiver of any other breach.

20.5 Counterparts; Electronic Signatures. This Agreement may be executed in counterparts, each of which is deemed an original. Signatures delivered electronically (including PDF or e-signature platform) are binding.

20.6 Governing Law. This Agreement is governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the State of California, without regard to conflict of law principles.

contract.title: Freelance Service Agreement (California) — Brand Refresh 2025
contract.preamble: This Freelance Service Agreement (the "Agreement") is entered into as of January 15, 2025 (the "Effective Date"), by and between TechStartup Inc., with its principal place of business at ______________________________ ("Client"), and Chen Creative Studio, with its principal place of business at ______________________________ ("Freelancer"). Client and Freelancer may be referred to individually as a "Party" and collectively as the "Parties." Client’s authorized representative is Sarah Johnson, Marketing Director. Freelancer’s authorized representative is Alex Chen, Senior Designer.

clauses.title: Services and Scope of Work
clauses.content:
1.1 Services. Freelancer shall provide brand design and visual identity redesign services for the project titled “Brand Refresh 2025” (the “Project”), including: (a) logo concepts; (b) selected logo refinement; (c) color palette and typography system; (d) brand guidelines document; and (e) delivery of final brand assets, as further described in this Agreement.

1.2 Scope. The scope includes creative development, design execution, reasonable project management and communications, and delivery of the Deliverables listed in Section 2. Any services not expressly included are outside scope and require a written change order under Section 9.

1.3 Client Cooperation. Client shall timely provide access to existing brand materials, preferences, approvals, and feedback reasonably required for Freelancer to perform the Services. Client acknowledges that delays in providing inputs/approvals may impact deadlines, and the schedule will be adjusted accordingly upon written notice by Freelancer.

clauses.title: Deliverables, Milestones, and Deadlines
clauses.content:
2.1 Deliverables and Milestone Due Dates. Freelancer shall deliver the following (collectively, the “Deliverables”) in accordance with the schedule below:

(a) Logo concepts (3 options) — due January 30, 2025 — Milestone Fee: $2,000.
(b) Selected logo refinement — due February 15, 2025 — Milestone Fee: $1,500.
(c) Brand guidelines document — due March 1, 2025 — Milestone Fee: $2,500.
(d) Asset package delivery — due March 15, 2025 — Milestone Fee: $2,500.

2.2 Deadline. The overall project deadline is March 15, 2025 (the “Deadline”), subject to extensions due to Client delays, Force Majeure, or approved change orders.

2.3 Delivery Method. Deliverables shall be provided digitally (e.g., secure link, email, or Client-approved file transfer) in commonly used professional formats. Unless otherwise agreed in writing, source/editable files are included only to the extent specified in the asset package in Section 2.1(d).

clauses.title: Compensation, Deposit, and Payment Schedule
clauses.content:
3.1 Total Fee. Client shall pay Freelancer a total of Eight Thousand Five Hundred Dollars ($8,500.00) (the “Total Amount”), as compensation for the Services and Deliverables described herein.

3.2 Deposit. A non-refundable deposit of Two Thousand Five Hundred Fifty Dollars ($2,550.00) is due upon execution of this Agreement and before work commences. The deposit will be credited against the Total Amount.

3.3 Milestone Payments. The remaining balance shall be paid on a milestone basis as follows (each, a “Milestone Payment”):

(a) $2,000 upon delivery of logo concepts (Section 2.1(a)), less any portion previously satisfied by the Deposit.
(b) $1,500 upon delivery of selected logo refinement (Section 2.1(b)).
(c) $2,500 upon delivery of brand guidelines document (Section 2.1(c)).
(d) $2,500 upon delivery of asset package delivery (Section 2.1(d)).

3.4 Application of Deposit. The Parties intend that the Deposit be applied first to amounts due under Section 3.3(a) and then, if any remainder, to subsequent milestones, unless otherwise specified in writing.

3.5 Invoicing and Due Dates. Freelancer may invoice upon completion/delivery of each milestone. Each invoice is due within fifteen (15) calendar days of the invoice date.

3.6 Late Payments. Past-due amounts shall accrue interest at the lesser of: (a) 1.0% per month; or (b) the maximum rate permitted by California law. Client shall reimburse reasonable costs of collection, including reasonable attorneys’ fees and costs, to the extent permitted by law.

3.7 Taxes. Freelancer is responsible for its own income and business taxes. Client shall not withhold payroll taxes or provide employee benefits.

clauses.title: Revisions, Review, and Acceptance
clauses.content:
4.1 Included Revisions. The Total Amount includes up to three (3) rounds of revisions (the “Revision Rounds”). A “round” means a single consolidated set of changes provided by Client after reviewing a delivered milestone.

4.2 Feedback Timing. Client shall provide feedback within five (5) business days of receipt of a milestone deliverable unless the Parties agree otherwise in writing. Failure to provide feedback within such period may extend the schedule.

4.3 Out-of-Scope Revisions. Requests exceeding the Revision Rounds, or requests constituting a change in direction, new concepts, or scope expansion, shall be billed as Additional Services under Section 9.

4.4 Acceptance. A milestone deliverable will be deemed accepted upon the earliest of: (a) Client’s written approval; (b) Client’s use of the deliverable in commerce or publication; or (c) five (5) business days after delivery without Client providing written rejection specifying material nonconformity with the agreed scope. If Client rejects a deliverable, Client must specify in writing the material nonconformity, and Freelancer will use commercially reasonable efforts to remedy within the scope.

clauses.title: Intellectual Property; Ownership; License; Portfolio
clauses.content:
5.1 Definitions.
(a) “Client Materials” means all trademarks, content, designs, copy, photographs, data, and other materials supplied by Client.
(b) “Work Product” means deliverables created by Freelancer specifically for Client under this Agreement.
(c) “Freelancer Tools” means Freelancer’s pre-existing materials, templates, know-how, processes, fonts, software, plug-ins, stock elements, and other tools not created specifically for Client.

5.2 Ownership Transfer (Conditioned on Payment). Upon Client’s full payment of all amounts due under this Agreement, including any Additional Services and reimbursable expenses, Freelancer assigns to Client all right, title, and interest in and to the Work Product, excluding Freelancer Tools and third-party materials, to the maximum extent permitted by law.

5.3 License Prior to Full Payment. Until full payment is received, Client receives a limited, revocable, non-exclusive, non-transferable license to internally review the Work Product solely for evaluation and approval purposes.

5.4 Freelancer Tools and Third-Party Materials.
(a) Freelancer Tools are and shall remain the property of Freelancer. To the extent any Freelancer Tools are embedded in the Work Product, Freelancer grants Client a perpetual, non-exclusive license to use such embedded Freelancer Tools solely as necessary to use the Work Product as delivered.
(b) Third-party materials (including fonts, stock photos, mockups, or software) may be subject to separate license terms and fees. Client is responsible for obtaining and complying with any required third-party licenses unless expressly included in writing.

5.5 Moral Rights. To the extent permitted by California law, Freelancer waives and agrees not to assert any “moral rights” or rights of attribution/integrity in the Work Product against Client or Client’s successors and assigns.

5.6 Portfolio and Publicity (Optional). Freelancer may display the Work Product and Client’s name/logo in Freelancer’s portfolio, website, social media, awards submissions, and marketing materials after Client publicly launches the new brand identity or after March 15, 2025, whichever occurs first. Client may request reasonable confidentiality for pre-launch materials under Section 6.

clauses.title: Confidentiality
clauses.content:
6.1 Confidential Information. “Confidential Information” means non-public information disclosed by a Party that is designated as confidential or that reasonably should be understood to be confidential, including business plans, marketing strategies, product plans, customer information, pricing, and non-public brand concepts.

6.2 Obligations. The receiving Party shall: (a) use Confidential Information only to perform or receive benefits under this Agreement; (b) not disclose it to any third party except to its employees/contractors with a need to know who are bound by confidentiality obligations at least as protective as this Agreement; and (c) protect it using reasonable care.

6.3 Exclusions. Confidential Information does not include information that: (a) is or becomes publicly available without breach; (b) was lawfully known to the receiving Party before disclosure; (c) is independently developed without use of the disclosing Party’s Confidential Information; or (d) is rightfully obtained from a third party without duty of confidentiality.

6.4 Compelled Disclosure. If compelled by law to disclose Confidential Information, the receiving Party shall provide prompt notice (if legally permitted) and reasonably cooperate to seek protective treatment.

6.5 Duration. These confidentiality obligations continue for three (3) years after termination or expiration of this Agreement; trade secrets remain protected for so long as they qualify as trade secrets under applicable law.

clauses.title: Independent Contractor Relationship
clauses.content:
7.1 Independent Contractor. Freelancer is an independent contractor and not an employee, agent, joint venturer, or partner of Client. Freelancer has no authority to bind Client.

7.2 Control of Work. Freelancer retains discretion over the manner and means of performing the Services, subject to the scope, deadlines, and requirements in this Agreement.

7.3 Subcontractors. Freelancer may use qualified subcontractors to perform portions of the Services, provided Freelancer remains responsible for performance and confidentiality compliance.

clauses.title: Warranties; Disclaimers
clauses.content:
8.1 Mutual Authority. Each Party represents it has the right and authority to enter into this Agreement.

8.2 Freelancer Standard of Care. Freelancer warrants that Services will be performed in a professional and workmanlike manner consistent with industry standards.

8.3 Client Materials. Client warrants it owns or has sufficient rights to use and provide the Client Materials and that Client Materials do not infringe third-party rights.

8.4 IP Non-Infringement Disclaimer. Freelancer does not provide legal clearance, trademark searches, or opinions regarding infringement. Client is responsible for obtaining trademark and legal clearance for any selected logo and brand elements.

8.5 Disclaimer. EXCEPT AS EXPRESSLY PROVIDED IN THIS AGREEMENT, FREELANCER DISCLAIMS ALL WARRANTIES, EXPRESS OR IMPLIED, INCLUDING ANY IMPLIED WARRANTIES OF MERCHANTABILITY, FITNESS FOR A PARTICULAR PURPOSE, AND NON-INFRINGEMENT, TO THE MAXIMUM EXTENT PERMITTED BY LAW.

clauses.title: Changes, Additional Services, and Expenses
clauses.content:
9.1 Change Requests. Any material change to scope, deliverables, deadlines, or assumptions must be documented in a written change order signed (including by email acknowledgment) by both Parties.

9.2 Additional Services Rates. Unless otherwise agreed in writing, Additional Services will be billed at $150/hour in 0.25-hour increments.

9.3 Expenses. No expenses will be charged without Client’s prior written approval. Approved expenses will be invoiced at cost and reimbursed within fifteen (15) days.

clauses.title: Term, Termination, and Kill Fee
clauses.content:
10.1 Term. This Agreement begins on the Effective Date and continues until completion of the Services, unless terminated earlier as provided herein.

10.2 Termination for Convenience. Either Party may terminate this Agreement for any reason upon seven (7) days’ written notice.

10.3 Termination for Cause. A Party may terminate immediately upon written notice if the other Party materially breaches this Agreement and fails to cure the breach within ten (10) days after written notice of breach.

10.4 Effect of Termination; Payment. Upon termination, Client shall pay Freelancer for: (a) all Services performed and milestones completed through the effective termination date; (b) authorized expenses incurred; and (c) a reasonable wind-down and file handoff time not to exceed three (3) hours unless otherwise agreed.

10.5 Deposit; Kill Fee. The Deposit is earned upon receipt and is non-refundable, and will be applied to amounts owed. If termination occurs after work has begun, and amounts owed for work performed exceed the Deposit, Client shall pay the difference.

10.6 Deliverables on Termination. Upon Client’s request and subject to Section 5 (payment condition), Freelancer will provide work-in-progress files and reasonably available materials created as of termination in their then-current form, with no guarantee of completeness or fitness.

clauses.title: Limitation of Liability
clauses.content:
11.1 Limitation. TO THE MAXIMUM EXTENT PERMITTED BY LAW, IN NO EVENT SHALL FREELANCER BE LIABLE FOR ANY INDIRECT, INCIDENTAL, SPECIAL, CONSEQUENTIAL, OR PUNITIVE DAMAGES (INCLUDING LOST PROFITS, LOST REVENUE, OR LOSS OF GOODWILL) ARISING OUT OF OR RELATED TO THIS AGREEMENT, EVEN IF ADVISED OF THE POSSIBILITY.

11.2 Cap. TO THE MAXIMUM EXTENT PERMITTED BY LAW, FREELANCER’S TOTAL CUMULATIVE LIABILITY ARISING OUT OF OR RELATED TO THIS AGREEMENT SHALL NOT EXCEED THE AMOUNTS PAID BY CLIENT TO FREELANCER UNDER THIS AGREEMENT IN THE THREE (3) MONTHS PRECEDING THE EVENT GIVING RISE TO THE CLAIM.

11.3 Exceptions. Nothing in this Agreement limits liability for: (a) a Party’s gross negligence or willful misconduct; (b) Client’s payment obligations; or (c) infringement arising from Client Materials supplied by Client.

clauses.title: Indemnification
clauses.content:
12.1 By Client. Client shall indemnify, defend, and hold harmless Freelancer and its officers, directors, employees, and contractors from and against any third-party claims, damages, liabilities, costs, and expenses (including reasonable attorneys’ fees) arising out of or relating to: (a) Client Materials; (b) Client’s use of the Work Product in combination with other materials not provided by Freelancer; or (c) Client’s modification of the Work Product.

12.2 By Freelancer. Freelancer shall indemnify and hold harmless Client and its officers, directors, and employees from and against any third-party claims that the Work Product, as delivered and used as intended (excluding Client Materials, Freelancer Tools, and third-party materials), infringes such third party’s U.S. copyright, provided that Client: (a) promptly notifies Freelancer in writing; (b) allows Freelancer sole control of defense and settlement; and (c) reasonably cooperates. Freelancer may, at its option: (i) procure a license; (ii) modify the Work Product to be non-infringing while substantially preserving function and appearance; or (iii) if (i) and (ii) are not commercially reasonable, refund to Client the amounts paid for the infringing portion of the Work Product, and this subsection states Client’s exclusive remedy for such infringement claim.

12.3 Exclusions. Freelancer has no indemnity obligation to the extent a claim arises from: (a) Client Materials; (b) Client’s instructions or required elements; (c) Client’s modifications; or (d) Client’s failure to obtain required third-party licenses (e.g., fonts/stock).

clauses.title: Dispute Resolution; Governing Law; Venue
clauses.content:
13.1 Good Faith Negotiation. The Parties shall first attempt in good faith to resolve any dispute through informal negotiations.

13.2 Optional Mediation. If unresolved, either Party may request mediation in California through a mutually agreed mediator, with costs shared equally.

13.3 Governing Law. This Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the State of California, without regard to conflict of laws principles.

13.4 Venue. Subject to Section 13.2, any action arising out of or relating to this Agreement shall be brought in the state or federal courts located in California, and each Party consents to such courts’ jurisdiction and venue.

clauses.title: Notices
clauses.content:
All notices required or permitted under this Agreement must be in writing and will be deemed given when delivered personally, sent by confirmed email, sent by nationally recognized overnight courier, or mailed by certified mail (return receipt requested) to the addresses below (or to any updated address a Party designates by notice).

Client Notices To:
TechStartup Inc.
Attn: Sarah Johnson, Marketing Director
Address: ______________________________
Email: ______________________________

Freelancer Notices To:
Chen Creative Studio
Attn: Alex Chen, Senior Designer
Address: ______________________________
Email: ______________________________

clauses.title: Miscellaneous
clauses.content:
15.1 Entire Agreement. This Agreement (including any exhibits or change orders) constitutes the entire agreement between the Parties regarding the subject matter and supersedes all prior or contemporaneous agreements and communications.

15.2 Amendment. Any amendment must be in writing and signed by both Parties.

15.3 Assignment. Neither Party may assign this Agreement without the other Party’s prior written consent, except to a successor in connection with a merger, acquisition, or sale of substantially all assets, provided the assignee assumes obligations herein.

15.4 Severability. If any provision is held invalid or unenforceable, the remaining provisions will remain in full force and effect.

15.5 Waiver. No waiver is effective unless in writing. A waiver of any breach is not a waiver of any other breach.

15.6 Counterparts; Electronic Signatures. This Agreement may be executed in counterparts and delivered electronically (including via PDF or e-signature platform), each of which will be deemed an original and all of which together constitute one instrument.

15.7 Headings. Headings are for convenience only and do not affect interpretation.

contract.title: Freelance Service Agreement
contract.preamble: This Freelance Service Agreement ("Agreement") is entered into and made effective as of January 15, 2025 ("Effective Date") by and between TechStartup Inc., a California corporation, on behalf of Client represented by Sarah Johnson, Marketing Director ("Client"), and Chen Creative Studio, operated by Alex Chen, Senior Designer ("Freelancer"). Client and Freelancer are each a "Party" and together the "Parties."
contract.recitals: WHEREAS, Client desires to retain Freelancer to perform design and branding services in connection with a project titled "Brand Refresh 2025"; and WHEREAS, Freelancer has the professional expertise and agrees to perform the services described herein under the terms and conditions set forth in this Agreement; NOW, THEREFORE, in consideration of the mutual covenants and promises contained herein, the Parties agree as follows:

clauses.title: Scope of Services
clauses.content: Freelancer will perform professional design services for Client for the project "Brand Refresh 2025," consisting of a complete visual identity redesign including logo, color palette, typography, and a brand guidelines document. Deliverables, due dates, and allocation of fees are: (a) Logo concepts (3 options) — due January 30, 2025 — $2,000; (b) Selected logo refinement — due February 15, 2025 — $1,500; (c) Brand guidelines document — due March 1, 2025 — $2,500; (d) Asset package delivery (final files for print and web, color specs, fonts/links, export formats) — due March 15, 2025 — $2,500. Freelancer shall provide up to three (3) rounds of revisions as set forth in Clause 6. Any work outside this scope constitutes a change order and will be billed in accordance with Clause 7.

clauses.title: Term and Project Deadline
clauses.content: The term of this Agreement commences on the Effective Date and will continue until completion of the Services and delivery of all Deliverables, unless earlier terminated under Clause 12. The overall project deadline for all Deliverables is March 15, 2025. Time is of the essence with respect to the deadlines set forth in this Agreement. Delivery dates may be adjusted only by written agreement of the Parties or as set forth in Clause 8 (Force Majeure).

clauses.title: Compensation and Payment Terms
clauses.content: Total fee for the Services is Eight Thousand Five Hundred Dollars ($8,500) ("Total Amount"). Client shall pay a non-refundable deposit of Two Thousand Five Hundred Fifty Dollars ($2,550) upon execution of this Agreement. The deposit shall be credited against invoices in the following manner: the deposit will first be applied to the first milestone(s) invoiced; any excess deposit balance will be applied to subsequent invoices. Freelancer will invoice the Client upon delivery of each milestone. Milestone amounts are allocated as follows: Logo concepts (3 options) — $2,000; Selected logo refinement — $1,500; Brand guidelines document — $2,500; Asset package delivery — $2,500. Payment for each invoice is due within seven (7) calendar days of receipt of invoice. Any amounts not paid when due shall accrue interest at the rate of 1.5% per month (or the maximum rate permitted by law, if less) from the due date until paid. If Client fails to pay any undisputed invoice within ten (10) days following written notice, Freelancer may suspend work until payment is received.

clauses.title: Deposit and Expenses
clauses.content: The deposit is non-refundable except as expressly provided herein. Client agrees to reimburse Freelancer for pre-approved out-of-pocket expenses reasonably incurred in connection with the Services (e.g., stock imagery, typeface licensing, third-party vendor fees). Such expenses will be itemized on invoices. Freelancer will obtain Client's prior written approval for any single expense in excess of Two Hundred Dollars ($200).

clauses.title: Revision Rounds
clauses.content: Freelancer will include up to three (3) rounds of revisions per Deliverable. A "round" means a single set of changes submitted by Client in response to a Deliverable. Revisions beyond the included rounds, or requests that materially change previously approved concepts, will be considered additional work and billed at Freelancer's standard hourly rate of One Hundred Dollars ($100) per hour, or at a mutually agreed fixed fee. Additional revision work will be commenced only after Client's written approval of the associated cost.

clauses.title: Change Orders
clauses.content: Any request by Client to modify the scope, schedule, or deliverables shall be submitted in writing and will be effective only if accepted by Freelancer in writing as a Change Order, which may include adjustments to fees, schedules, and deliverables. Freelancer is not required to perform services beyond the Scope of Services without an executed Change Order.

clauses.title: Client Responsibilities and Approvals
clauses.content: Client shall provide timely access to information, materials, approvals, and feedback reasonably required by Freelancer to perform the Services. Client’s failure to respond or approve in a timely manner may result in schedule delays and additional fees. Client represents that any materials it provides do not infringe third-party rights. Client shall be responsible for obtaining any necessary permissions and licenses for materials provided to Freelancer.

clauses.title: Delivery; Acceptance
clauses.content: Freelancer will deliver the Deliverables in electronic format to Client at the agreed milestones. Client will review delivered work and provide any requested feedback or approval within five (5) business days. Failure to respond within five (5) business days shall be deemed acceptance. Upon Client acceptance and full payment of all fees due for a Deliverable (including any outstanding invoices), ownership and rights shall transfer as set forth in Clause 10.

clauses.title: Ownership, Copyright, and License
clauses.content: Subject to Client's full payment of all fees and expenses due under this Agreement, Freelancer assigns to Client all right, title, and interest in and to the final Deliverables created specifically for Client under this Agreement, including copyrights ("Assigned IP"). Freelancer retains ownership of and all rights in Freelancer's pre-existing materials, tools, templates, methodologies, and any third-party materials not owned by Freelancer (collectively, "Retained Materials"). To the extent any Retained Materials are included in the Deliverables, Freelancer grants Client a non-exclusive, worldwide, perpetual license to use such Retained Materials as incorporated into the Deliverables for Client's business purposes. Freelancer reserves the right to include Deliverables in Freelancer's portfolio, marketing materials, and website unless Client provides written objection prior to execution. If moral rights exist under applicable law, Freelancer hereby waives and agrees not to assert such rights to the maximum extent permitted by law.

clauses.title: Warranties and Representations
clauses.content: Each Party represents and warrants that it has the power and authority to enter into this Agreement. Freelancer represents that the Deliverables will be original works prepared by Freelancer and will not knowingly infringe the intellectual property rights of any third party. Except as expressly provided, FREELANCER DISCLAIMS ALL OTHER WARRANTIES, EXPRESS OR IMPLIED, INCLUDING WARRANTIES OF MERCHANTABILITY OR FITNESS FOR A PARTICULAR PURPOSE, TO THE MAXIMUM EXTENT PERMITTED BY LAW.

clauses.title: Indemnification
clauses.content: Client shall indemnify, defend and hold Freelancer harmless from and against any losses, damages, liabilities, claims, costs and expenses (including reasonable attorneys' fees) arising out of Client's use of the Deliverables, Client's breach of this Agreement, or Client-provided materials that infringe third-party rights. Freelancer shall indemnify, defend and hold Client harmless from and against any losses, damages, liabilities, claims, costs and expenses (including reasonable attorneys' fees) arising from a claim that the Deliverables, as delivered by Freelancer and used as authorized herein, infringe a third party's copyright, provided Client gives Freelancer prompt written notice of such claim and cooperates in the defense.

clauses.title: Limitation of Liability
clauses.content: EXCEPT FOR A PARTY'S INDEMNIFICATION OBLIGATIONS OR WILLFUL MISCONDUCT, IN NO EVENT WILL EITHER PARTY BE LIABLE TO THE OTHER FOR CONSEQUENTIAL, INCIDENTAL, EXEMPLARY, PUNITIVE OR SPECIAL DAMAGES, INCLUDING LOST PROFITS, ARISING OUT OF OR RELATING TO THIS AGREEMENT, WHETHER IN CONTRACT, TORT OR OTHERWISE. FREELANCER'S AGGREGATE LIABILITY FOR ANY CLAIM ARISING OUT OF OR RELATED TO THIS AGREEMENT SHALL NOT EXCEED THE TOTAL FEES PAID BY CLIENT TO FREELANCER UNDER THIS AGREEMENT.

clauses.title: Confidentiality
clauses.content: Each Party shall hold in confidence and not disclose the other Party's confidential information, including pricing, business practices, product development, trade secrets, and any non-public information designated as confidential or that reasonably should be understood to be confidential. Confidential information does not include information that: (a) is or becomes publicly known through no breach by the receiving Party; (b) is rightfully received from a third party without restriction; (c) is independently developed by the receiving Party; or (d) is required to be disclosed by law or court order (provided prompt notice to the disclosing Party). The obligations of confidentiality survive termination of this Agreement for three (3) years.

clauses.title: Termination
clauses.content: Either Party may terminate this Agreement for material breach by the other Party if the breaching Party fails to cure the breach within ten (10) calendar days after written notice. Client may terminate for convenience upon fourteen (14) calendar days' prior written notice. Upon termination, Client shall pay Freelancer for all Services performed and expenses incurred up to the effective date of termination, including any non-cancellable commitments, and Freelancer shall deliver to Client all work-in-progress. The deposit is non-refundable except to the extent Freelancer returns unearned fees as calculated by pro rata allocation of the Total Amount less work already performed. Sections regarding Payment, Ownership, Indemnification, Limitation of Liability, Confidentiality, Governing Law, Dispute Resolution, and Survival shall survive termination.

clauses.title: Force Majeure
clauses.content: Neither Party shall be liable for delay or failure to perform due to causes beyond its reasonable control, including acts of God, natural disasters, pandemics, labor disputes, government action, or failure of suppliers, provided the affected Party gives prompt notice and uses reasonable efforts to mitigate the delay. If a force majeure event continues for more than thirty (30) days, either Party may terminate this Agreement with written notice and Client shall pay for Services performed through the termination date.

clauses.title: Dispute Resolution; Governing Law and Venue
clauses.content: The Parties shall first attempt to resolve any dispute arising under or related to this Agreement through good-faith negotiation. If the Parties cannot resolve the dispute within thirty (30) days, they agree to attempt mediation in California. If mediation fails, any unresolved controversy or claim shall be resolved by binding arbitration under the rules of the American Arbitration Association (or another mutually agreed arbitration provider) conducted in San Francisco County, California. Judgment upon the award may be entered in any court of competent jurisdiction. This Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the State of California, without regard to its conflicts of law principles.

clauses.title: Independent Contractor; Taxes
clauses.content: Freelancer is an independent contractor and not an employee, agent, or partner of Client. Freelancer is solely responsible for all taxes, withholdings, benefits, and insurance related to amounts paid under this Agreement. Nothing in this Agreement shall be construed to create an employment relationship.

clauses.title: Insurance
clauses.content: During the term of this Agreement, Freelancer shall maintain commercially reasonable insurance coverage appropriate for the Services, including general liability insurance. Upon Client's request, Freelancer will provide proof of insurance to Client.

clauses.title: Publicity
clauses.content: Freelancer may identify Client as a client and may display the Deliverables in Freelancer's portfolio, website, and marketing materials unless Client provides written objection at the time of signing. If Client objects, Parties will negotiate a reasonable alternative.

clauses.title: Non-Solicitation
clauses.content: During the term of this Agreement and for a period of twelve (12) months thereafter, Client agrees not to directly solicit or hire Freelancer personnel who substantially participated in performance of the Services without Freelancer's prior written consent. This provision shall not apply to general solicitations not specifically directed at Freelancer employees or contractors.

clauses.title: Notices
clauses.content: All notices required or permitted under this Agreement shall be in writing and shall be deemed given when delivered personally, sent by certified mail (return receipt requested), or by nationally recognized overnight courier to the addresses set forth below or such other addresses as may be designated by written notice: Client: Sarah Johnson, Marketing Director, TechStartup Inc.; Freelancer: Alex Chen, Chen Creative Studio. Electronic notices (email) shall be effective when receipt is confirmed by the receiving Party.

clauses.title: Severability; Amendment; Entire Agreement
clauses.content: If any provision of this Agreement is held invalid or unenforceable, the remaining provisions shall remain in full force and effect. This Agreement may be amended only by a writing signed by both Parties. This Agreement constitutes the entire agreement between the Parties with respect to the subject matter hereof and supersedes all prior and contemporaneous agreements and understandings, whether written or oral.

clauses.title: Counterparts; Electronic Signatures
clauses.content: This Agreement may be executed in counterparts, each of which shall be deemed an original and all of which together shall constitute one instrument. Signatures transmitted by electronic means (e.g., PDF or facsimile) shall be deemed original signatures for all purposes.

contract.title: Freelance Service Agreement
contract.preamble: This Freelance Service Agreement ("Agreement") is entered into on 2025-01-15 ("Effective Date") by and between TechStartup Inc., a corporation having a principal place of business in California, represented by Sarah Johnson, Marketing Director ("Client"), and Chen Creative Studio, represented by Alex Chen, Senior Designer ("Freelancer").
contract.recitals: WHEREAS, Client desires to engage Freelancer to perform design services in connection with Client's project titled "Brand Refresh 2025"; and WHEREAS, Freelancer has the skills, experience, and ability to perform such services and desires to provide the services to Client under the terms set forth herein; NOW, THEREFORE, in consideration of the mutual covenants contained herein, the parties agree as follows:

clauses.title: Scope of Services
clauses.content: Freelancer shall provide a complete visual identity redesign for Client's "Brand Refresh 2025," including logo concepts, logo refinement, a brand guidelines document, and an asset package (collectively, the "Services"). The Services shall conform to the descriptions, deliverables, and due dates set forth in Clause 3 (Deliverables and Schedule). Any work beyond the Scope of Services shall require a written change order agreed by both parties and may incur additional fees.

clauses.title: Term
clauses.content: This Agreement commences on the Effective Date and continues until completion of the Services or termination in accordance with Clause 11. The Services shall be completed by 2025-03-15 ("Deadline"), subject to adjustments by mutual written agreement or delay caused by Client or Force Majeure.

clauses.title: Deliverables and Schedule
clauses.content:
Freelancer will deliver the following deliverables according to the schedule and amounts shown: 
- Logo concepts (3 options) — due Jan 30, 2025 — $2,000
- Selected logo refinement — due Feb 15, 2025 — $1,500
- Brand guidelines document — due Mar 1, 2025 — $2,500
- Asset package delivery — due Mar 15, 2025 — $2,500
Each deliverable shall include the file formats and resolution reasonably required for Client's intended use as discussed between the parties. Timely Client feedback is required to meet scheduled dates.

clauses.title: Fees and Payment Terms
clauses.content: Total Fee: $8,500. Client shall pay a non‑refundable deposit of $2,550 (30% of Total Fee) upon execution of this Agreement. Remaining payments shall be made on a milestone basis as follows: payment for Logo concepts — $2,000 due upon delivery of that milestone; payment for Selected logo refinement — $1,500 due upon delivery of that milestone; payment for Brand guidelines document — $2,500 due upon delivery of that milestone; payment for Asset package delivery — $2,500 due upon delivery of that milestone. Freelancer will invoice Client for each milestone upon delivery; invoices are payable within 15 days of receipt unless otherwise agreed in writing. Late payments shall accrue interest at the lesser of 1.5% per month or the maximum rate permitted by law. All amounts are payable in U.S. dollars.

clauses.title: Revisions
clauses.content: The fee includes up to three (3) rounds of reasonable revision for each deliverable. Additional revisions requested by Client beyond the included three rounds will be billed at Freelancer's standard hourly rate (if not pre-stated, to be agreed in writing prior to performing such additional work). A revision round is a single cycle of Client feedback followed by Freelancer's updated submission.

clauses.title: Client Responsibilities
clauses.content: Client shall provide timely feedback, approvals, source materials, brand content, and any other information reasonably necessary for Freelancer to perform the Services. If Client fails to respond within five (5) business days to requests for approvals or information, Freelancer may treat the last set of written instructions as approved and may invoice for work performed. Delays caused by Client may result in schedule adjustments and additional fees.

clauses.title: Ownership, Copyright, and License
clauses.content: Subject to Client's full payment of all amounts due under this Agreement, Freelancer assigns to Client all right, title, and interest in and to the final delivered deliverables ("Final Deliverables") created specifically for Client under this Agreement, excluding Freelancer's preexisting materials, general concepts, ideas, templates, tools, software, or methodologies (collectively, "Freelancer Materials"). Freelancer retains all right, title, and interest in Freelancer Materials. Freelancer grants Client a perpetual, worldwide, royalty‑free license to use any Freelancer Materials included in the Final Deliverables to the extent necessary for Client's use of the Final Deliverables. Freelancer may display the Final Deliverables and work‑in‑progress in Freelancer's portfolio and for self-promotion unless Client provides written notice that certain materials are confidential trade secrets (subject to Clause 10).

clauses.title: Warranties and Representations
clauses.content: Each party represents and warrants that it has the full power and authority to enter into this Agreement. Freelancer warrants that the Final Deliverables delivered to Client are original to Freelancer or properly licensed for use by Client and that to Freelancer's knowledge, use of the Final Deliverables by Client in accordance with this Agreement will not infringe upon the intellectual property rights of any third party. EXCEPT AS EXPRESSLY SET FORTH HEREIN, FREELANCER DISCLAIMS ALL OTHER WARRANTIES, EXPRESS OR IMPLIED, INCLUDING ANY WARRANTIES OF MERCHANTABILITY OR FITNESS FOR A PARTICULAR PURPOSE.

clauses.title: Indemnification
clauses.content: Client shall indemnify, defend and hold Freelancer harmless from and against any losses, liabilities, damages and expenses (including reasonable attorneys' fees) arising out of Client's use of the Final Deliverables in a manner not authorized by this Agreement or based on Client-supplied materials. Freelancer shall indemnify, defend and hold Client harmless from and against any losses, liabilities, damages and expenses (including reasonable attorneys' fees) arising from Freelancer's breach of Clause 8 (Warranties) or from Freelancer's willful infringement of a third party's intellectual property rights in the delivered work. The indemnifying party's obligations are conditioned upon the indemnified party providing prompt written notice of a claim, reasonable cooperation, and the ability to control the defense and settlement of the claim.

clauses.title: Confidentiality
clauses.content: Each party shall keep confidential and not disclose to any third party any nonpublic proprietary or confidential information of the other party disclosed in connection with this Agreement ("Confidential Information"), except as required by law or with the disclosing party's prior written consent. Confidential Information does not include information that is or becomes publicly known through no wrongful act of the receiving party or information independently developed by the receiving party without reference to the disclosing party's Confidential Information. Upon request, Confidential Information (other than deliverables already paid for and delivered) will be returned or destroyed.

clauses.title: Termination
clauses.content: Either party may terminate this Agreement for material breach by the other party if the breaching party fails to cure the breach within 14 days of written notice. Client may terminate for convenience upon 7 days' written notice; in such event, Freelancer shall be paid for all Services performed and expenses incurred through the effective date of termination, including a reasonable portion of partially completed work. If Client fails to pay amounts when due, Freelancer may suspend Services until payment is made. Termination does not relieve Client of the obligation to pay for Services rendered prior to termination.

clauses.title: Limitation of Liability
clauses.content: IN NO EVENT WILL EITHER PARTY BE LIABLE TO THE OTHER FOR ANY INDIRECT, INCIDENTAL, CONSEQUENTIAL, SPECIAL OR PUNITIVE DAMAGES ARISING OUT OF OR RELATING TO THIS AGREEMENT, EVEN IF ADVISED OF THE POSSIBILITY OF SUCH DAMAGES. THE AGGREGATE LIABILITY OF EITHER PARTY FOR ANY CLAIM ARISING OUT OF OR RELATING TO THIS AGREEMENT SHALL NOT EXCEED THE TOTAL AMOUNTS PAID BY CLIENT TO FREELANCER UNDER THIS AGREEMENT.

clauses.title: Independent Contractor
clauses.content: Freelancer is an independent contractor and nothing in this Agreement creates an employer-employee, partnership, joint venture, or agency relationship. Freelancer is responsible for payment of all federal, state, and local taxes and any other statutory obligations arising from payments made to Freelancer.

clauses.title: Taxes
clauses.content: Client will not withhold any taxes from payments to Freelancer. Freelancer shall be solely responsible for all taxes, assessments, and other charges arising from payments made under this Agreement.

clauses.title: Force Majeure
clauses.content: Neither party shall be liable for delays or failures in performance resulting from causes beyond its reasonable control, including acts of God, government action, strikes, pandemics, civil unrest, or failure of suppliers, provided the affected party gives prompt written notice and uses commercially reasonable efforts to resume performance.

clauses.title: Dispute Resolution; Governing Law
clauses.content: This Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the State of California without regard to its conflicts of law principles. The parties shall first attempt in good faith to resolve any dispute arising out of or relating to this Agreement through negotiation. If negotiation fails, the parties agree to submit the dispute to mediation in California before initiating arbitration or litigation. If mediation does not resolve the dispute, either party may seek relief in a court of competent jurisdiction in California. Notwithstanding the foregoing, either party may seek injunctive or other equitable relief to protect its intellectual property rights.

clauses.title: Amendments and Waiver
clauses.content: Any amendment or modification to this Agreement must be in writing and signed by both parties. No waiver of any breach shall be deemed a waiver of any subsequent breach.

clauses.title: Severability
clauses.content: If any provision of this Agreement is held to be invalid, illegal, or unenforceable by a court of competent jurisdiction, the remaining provisions shall remain in full force and effect and the parties shall negotiate in good faith to replace the invalid provision with a valid provision that achieves, to the extent possible, the original intent.

clauses.title: Notices
clauses.content: All notices required or permitted under this Agreement shall be in writing and delivered by hand, nationally recognized overnight courier, certified mail (return receipt requested), or email (with confirmation of receipt) to the addresses listed below or to such other address as a party may designate by written notice. Notice is effective upon receipt.

clauses.title: Entire Agreement
clauses.content: This Agreement, including any exhibits or attachments and any written change orders signed by both parties, constitutes the entire agreement between the parties with respect to the subject matter hereof and supersedes all prior and contemporaneous understandings, agreements, or representations, whether written or oral.

clauses.title: Counterparts and Electronic Signatures
clauses.content: This Agreement may be executed in counterparts, each of which shall be deemed an original but all of which together shall constitute one and the same instrument. Signatures delivered by electronic means (e.g., PDF, fax, or electronic signature service) shall be deemed original signatures.
